data.title: Ageas to buy UK-based esure from Bain Capital for £1.295B
Oil prices to fall through 2026 on supply glut – Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Goldman Sachs expects oil prices to decline through the end of 2025 and into 2026, with Brent (CO1:COM) and WTI (CL1:COM) averaging $63 and $59 per barrel, respectively, this year and falling to $58 and $55 next year, due to rising recession risks and increased OPEC+ supply.

The bank projects oil demand will increase by just 300,000 barrels per day between the end of 2024 and the end of 2025, amid a weak growth outlook driven by a global trade war.

Due to the escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and China, the bank has lowered its global oil demand growth forecast for the fourth quarter of 2026 by 900,000 barrels per day since mid-March. China on Friday announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12.

Goldman Sachs predicts that even with some future inventory builds already factored in, substantial surpluses of 800,000 barrels per day in 2025 and 1.4 million barrels per day in 2026 will keep oil prices under pressure.

West Texas Intermediate (CL1:COM) crude futures was up +0.16% to $61.60 at the time of writing, while Brent crude (CO1:COM), the international benchmark, was +0.06% at $64.80 a barrel and has declined over 13% YTD.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (LE)

data.title: CK Hutchison ports: Italian billionaire's business said to emerge as lead investor
data.description:
A port operator, majority owned by Italian billionaire Gianluigi Aponte's family-run shipping company, is emerging as the lead investor of the buyer group for CK Hutchison's (OTCPK:CKHUY) 43 ports, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg. 

Terminal Investment Ltd ((TiL)) is expected to be the sole owner of all the ports, except for the two along the Panama Canal. TiL is 70% owned by Aponte's Mediterranean Shipping Co, 20% by BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) and 10% by Singapore's sovereign wealth fund.

BlackRock's (NYSE:BLK) unit Global Infrastructure Partners will likely own 51% of the two ports in Panama, while TiL would own the remaining stake. The two ports account for about 4% of the deal's total value. 

The consortium's structure was not explained when the deal was first announced last month. However, deliberations are still ongoing and the final structure could still change.

The deal faces an uphill regulatory battle, as the Chinese government is unhappy with the agreement and Panama's top auditor has accused CK Hutchison (OTCPK:CKHUY) of unpaid fees.

data.title: Europe indexes start week with strong gains after Trump tariff exemptions on tech
data.description:
London (UKX) +1.92% to 8,117. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +2.29% to 20,834.

France (CAC:IND) +2.11% to 7,254.

Switzerland’s producer and import prices edged down by 0.1 Y/Y in March.

Hungary's construction output declined by 4.5% Y/Y in February.

The annual inflation rate in Finland held steady at 0.5% in March.

The Netherlands’ trade surplus narrowed to €10.92B in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 2.04% higher to 496.98, with all sectors in green, bolstered by technology gains, after Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China. Meanwhile, investor focus is also turning to EU-US trade talks, as EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic is reportedly set to meet with U.S. officials.

Top gainers for the day include Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB), Infineon (OTCQX:IFNNY), Sartorius (OTCPK:SARTF), and Heidelberg Materials (OTCPK:HDLMY). 

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.47%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.55%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 4.71%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Spark Energy raises $1.85M via private placement
Sony increases costs of PS5 in Europe, Australia, and New Zealand amid tariff risks
data.description:
• Sony Group (NYSE:SONY) has increased the price of its PlayStation 5 console by around 25% in Europe and the UK.
  • The PS5 will cost €500 (RM2,516) in Europe and £430 (RM2,494) in the UK from April 14.
  • The company announced the decision on its PlayStation blog, with Australia and New Zealand also affected, citing inflation and "a backdrop of a challenging economic environment.”
  • The PS5 Pro, on the other hand, remains untouched.
  • The external disc drive for the PS5, meanwhile, is getting cheaper. The new price for that accessory drops to €79.99, £69.99, AUD $124.95, or NZD $139.95 depending on the region.

data.title: Olam plans to allocate $2B from Saudi sale to reduce debt
China suspends rare earth exports as trade war with U.S. escalates - report
data.description:
China has suspended rare earth exports in retaliation to President Trump’s recent tariff hikes, The New York Times reported.

The Chinese government is drafting a new regulatory system for exports, and while the policies are being framed, the shipments of the magnets, essential for the manufacturing of almost everything ranging from cars to missiles, have been halted at many Chinese ports, according to the report.

On April 4, China imposed new restrictions on the export of six heavy rare earth elements—metals that are refined exclusively in China—as well as rare earth magnets.

Beijing produces around 90% of the world's rare earths, a group of 17 elements used across the defense, electric vehicle, energy and electronics industries.

Meanwhile, China's exports of rare earths rose 20.31% in March from a year earlier as overseas consumers booked more cargoes, amid supply disruptions from the ongoing conflict in major supplier Myanmar.

China's exports of the group of 17 minerals stood at 5,666.3 metric tons last month, Reuters reported, citing data from the General Administration of Customs.

data.title: Torchlight Innovations and Innovation Mining reach definitive pact for proposed merger
data.description:
Torchlight Innovations (TLX.P:CA) has announced a definitive agreement to merge with Innovation Mining Inc., a clean-tech firm developing a non-toxic gold extraction technology called RZOLV.

The deal, structured as a "three-cornered amalgamation," positions Torchlight to exit its capital pool company status and acquire all the issued and outstanding securities of Innovation.

Under the "three-cornered amalgamation," Torchlight will merge its wholly-owned subsidiary (Subco) with Innovation Mining, creating a new entity (Amalco) that becomes Torchlight’s wholly-owned subsidiary. 

Upon completion of the merger, the resulting issuer will take over the business of Innovation as a Tier 2 technology Issuer.

In connection with the deal, Torchlight intends to consolidate its common shares on a 1.93:1 basis and change its name to "Innovation Mining" or such other name as may be determined by Innovation, the company added.

Following consolidation, Innovation Mining's owners will receive 55,011,000 shares of the new business in return for their holdings, as well as a slew of warrants and options.

The deal is supported by two finance components: SR Financing: A $2 million non-brokered private placement of subscription receipts convertible into shares and warrants after the transaction; and PP Financing: A $752,500 private placement of units (shares and warrants) priced at $0.35 pre-split ($0.28 post-split).
